question.text: The open offer under SAST must be made to all shareholders of the target company EXCEPT:

answer.text: The acquirer, persons acting in concert, and parties to the underlying agreement
Shareholders holding less than 1% of total shares

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The open offer must be made to ALL shareholders except the acquirer, persons acting in concert (PAC), and parties to the underlying share purchase agreement. These parties are excluded because they are already on the acquirer's side of the transaction. **The Trap:** "Institutional shareholders and mutual funds" are NOT excluded — they have the same right to tender as any other shareholder. The exclusion is based on the party's role in the acquisition, not their investor category. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX and NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) both cover the rights of institutional investors in takeover situations. Mutual funds can and do tender shares in open offers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The universality of the open offer is a key shareholder protection — every non-acquirer shareholder gets an equal exit opportunity at the same price, regardless of holding size or investor type.
